title: zinnia acerosa
intro: Zinnia acerosa, also known as the sticky zinnia, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant's stems and leaves contain a sticky resin that has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Zinnia acerosa has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to stimulate the production of mucus and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Zinnia acerosa include teas, infusions, and topical applications, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations.
